steps.title: Identify the key prediction
steps.content: The prediction: males (who disperse) show lower maternal dietary overlap than females (who stay).

steps.title: Match the dual comparison to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B compares both metrics for individual 17 (male) and individual 5 (female), showing smaller mother-offspring overlap for 17 and a larger unshared portion of 17's diet, exactly as predicted.

Choices A and D examine only one metric, while C misstates the data by claiming more than half of individual 17's mother's diet was shared (the graph shows only about 10%).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
